children.label: A
children.text: the democratically-elected National Assembly of Venezuela; or

children.label: B
children.text: the President of such National Assembly, while serving as Interim President of Venezuela, or the senior government officials under the supervision of such President;
is the spouse or adult child of a foreign person described in paragraph (1); or

children.label: A
children.text: section 5(a) of the Venezuela Defense of Human Rights and Civil Society Act of 2014 (), as amended by ;Public Law 113–278section 163 of this title2See References in Text note below.2

children.heading: Removal from visa revocation list
children.label: b Removal from visa revocation list

children.text: if any person described in subsection (a)(1) recognizes and pledges support for the Interim President of Venezuela or a subsequent democratically elected government of Venezuela, that person and any family members of that person who were subject to visa restrictions pursuant to subsection (a)(2) shall no longer be subject to such visa restrictions; and

if any person described in subparagraphs (A) through (C) of subsection (a)(3) recognizes and pledges support for the Interim President of Venezuela or a subsequent democratically elected government of Venezuela, any family members of that person who were subject to visa restrictions pursuant to subsection (a)(3) shall no longer be subject to such visa restrictions.

children.label: B
children.text: ineligible to receive a visa or other documentation to enter the United States; and

children.label: C
children.text: otherwise ineligible to be admitted or paroled into the United States or to receive any benefit under the Immigration and Nationality Act ( et seq.).8 U.S.C. 1101

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: An alien described in subsection (a) is subject to revocation of any visa or other entry documentation regardless of when the visa or other entry documentation is or was issued.

children.label: ii
children.text: automatically cancel any other valid visa or entry documentation that is in the alien’s possession.

children.label: A
children.text: to permit the United States to comply with the Agreement regarding the Headquarters of the United Nations, signed at Lake Success , and entered into force , between the United Nations and the United States, or other applicable international obligations; orJune 26, 1947November 21, 1947

children.label: B
children.text: to carry out or assist law enforcement activity in the United States.

children.heading: Rulemaking
children.label: d Rulemaking
children.text: The President shall issue such regulations, licenses, and orders as may be necessary to carry out this section.
